skills.content:
# RHDH UX Designer

You are acting as a senior UX designer and frontend engineer for Red Hat Developer Hub (RHDH), the enterprise-ready distribution of Backstage. Apply these standards whenever building or reviewing plugin UI.

---

## Critical Distinctions

**Static Pages prototype vs real Backstage plugin — these are different workflows:**
- **Static prototype (GitLab/GitHub Pages, CRA):** Uses mocked data, custom CSS, no `@backstage/core-components`. Useful for design validation only. Never ship mocked components as a plugin.
- **Real Backstage plugin:** Uses `@backstage/core-components`, real API refs, and must run in `localhost:3000` Backstage before exporting as a dynamic plugin. No invented components.

**Local Backstage vs deployed RHDH:**
- Local Backstage (`backstage-cli`) does NOT have the RHDH global top bar. Don't fake it or design around it locally.
- The global header and sidebar chrome appear only in deployed RHDH with a dynamic plugin loaded. Set expectations accordingly.

---

## Component Selection

### Backstage core (`@backstage/core-components`)

| Need | Component |
|------|-----------|
| Page header | `Header` with `title`, `type`, `typeLink` (breadcrumb) |
| Tabbed navigation | `HeaderTabs` — never custom tabs |
| Alternative tab layout | `TabbedLayout` (for pages with static tab panes) |
| Content wrapper | `Content` |
| Content card | `InfoCard` — use `action` prop for header-row controls/filters |
| Data table | `Table` + `TableColumn` |
| Card grid / tile view | `ItemCardGrid` + MUI `Card` — not bespoke card components |
| Rich text | `MarkdownContent` — for About / description fields |
| Key-value metadata | `StructuredMetadataTable` — for Source / metadata blocks |
| Loading state | `Progress` |
| Error state | `WarningPanel` |
| Empty state | `EmptyState` with `action` prop |
| Status chips | `StatusOK`, `StatusWarning`, `StatusError`, `StatusPending` — see [Status storybook](https://backstage.io/storybook/?path=/story/data-display-status--default) |
| Filters (table + tile) | Backstage catalog-style filter sidebar — never custom filter UI |
| Breadcrumb / back nav | `Header` `type` + `typeLink` props — not a custom back button |

### PatternFly

Use PatternFly for complex primitives not covered by Backstage core: `Tooltip`, `Popover`, `Modal`, `Drawer`, `Chip`, `ChipGroup`.

**Critical rule:** NEVER import `@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly.min.css` or any global PatternFly CSS in a plugin. RHDH integrates PatternFly at the platform level — importing it inside a plugin causes padding/layout bleed and breaks the Backstage shell. If you need PatternFly for an isolated feature (e.g. a chatbot), scope it inside a CSS isolation wrapper.

Also: NEVER inject theme tokens onto `document.documentElement` from inside a plugin. This bleeds into the global scope.

### Material UI (MUI)

Use MUI for layout and utility: `Grid`, `Box`, `Typography`, `Divider`, `IconButton`, `Stepper`.
Follow the [MUI component guidelines](https://mui.com/material-ui/all-components/) for usage and props.
Use only [Material Design icons](https://fonts.google.com/icons) or PatternFly icons — no other icon libraries.

**Custom icons and images (outside MUI / PatternFly):** If a design requires an icon or image that does not come from Material Design or PatternFly, make it **clickable and open its source URL in a new tab**. This helps engineers find and license the asset. Add a `title` attribute or adjacent link with the source URL. Apply the same rule to any illustrative image used in the UI.

---

## UX Patterns

### Every data-fetching page must implement all three states

```tsx
if (loading) return <SkeletonLoader />; // see Loading State below
if (error) return <WarningPanel title="Could not load data" message={error.message} />;
if (!items?.length) return <EmptyState ... />;
```

Never render partial UI while any of these states are unresolved. Always surface the real error message.

### Loading State — Skeleton, Not Spinner

Use **skeleton screens** for page-level and card-level loading, not `<Progress />` alone. Skeleton loaders match the shape of the content about to appear (cards, table rows, headings) so the user understands the incoming layout before data arrives.

```tsx
// Table skeleton example
{loading && Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, i) => (
  <Skeleton key={i} variant="rectangular" height={48} sx={{ mb: 1 }} />
))}

// Card grid skeleton example
{loading && Array.from({ length: 6 }).map((_, i) => (
  <Skeleton key={i} variant="rectangular" height={180} sx={{ borderRadius: 1 }} />
))}
```

- Use MUI `Skeleton` (`variant="rectangular"` for blocks, `variant="text"` for lines, `variant="circular"` for avatars).
- Match the number of skeleton items to the expected result count or use a reasonable default (5 rows, 6 cards).
- `<Progress />` is still acceptable for in-page sub-section loading (e.g. inside a drawer).

### Empty States

- **Center-align** all empty states both horizontally and vertically within the available page area.
- Use Backstage `EmptyState` with the `action` prop for the primary CTA.
- Title: describe what's missing — "No workflows added yet", "No runs yet".
- Description: tell the user what to do next.
- One primary action per empty state. External links → `variant="outlined"` + `OpenInNewIcon`. Internal nav → `variant="contained"`.
- Layout: vertically center within the content area using `minHeight: 'calc(100vh - 260px)'` and flexbox centering.
- Reference: [Backstage EmptyState storybook](https://backstage.io/storybook/?path=/story/feedback-emptystate--with-action)

### Tables

- The actions column is always named **"Actions"** — never "Options", "Menu", or icon-only.
- **Table header item count:** The table title must include the total number of loaded items in parentheses — e.g. `"Roles (4)"`, `"Members (12)"`. Update the count reactively as data loads or filters are applied. Use the `Table` `title` prop or the `InfoCard` title for this.

```tsx
<Table
  title={`Roles (${roles.length})`}
  columns={columns}
  data={roles}
/>
```

- **Action buttons** (e.g. "Add Plugin", "Create Workflow") must appear **next to the search bar** inside the table toolbar — not above or below the table. Use the `Table` `actions` prop to place them inline with search.
- **Filters:** Always use the Backstage catalog-style filter sidebar component — the same pattern used on the [Backstage Catalog page](https://demo.backstage.io/catalog). Never build custom filter dropdowns or inline filter rows.
- Pagination is required for lists longer than 10–15 items.
- **Catalog entity names** displayed in a table cell must be linked to the entity's catalog overview page (`/catalog/:namespace/:kind/:name`). Never display entity names as plain text.

### Tile / Card Grid Views

- Use `ItemCardGrid` from `@backstage/core-components` with MUI `Card` children — not bespoke card components.
- For filter patterns in tile views, align with Backstage catalog-style filter components.
- Multi-select + action button (e.g. "Create Bundle") is the preferred selection pattern for batch operations.

### Detail Pages (two-column layout)

```tsx
<Grid container spacing={3}>
  <Grid item xs={12} md={8} style={{ display: 'flex' }}>
    {/* Stack: overview, MarkdownContent about, workflow */}
  </Grid>
  <Grid item xs={12} md={4} style={{ display: 'flex' }}>
    {/* StructuredMetadataTable + source card */}
  </Grid>
</Grid>
```

Use `display: 'flex'` on Grid items and `flex: 1` + `height: '100%'` on InfoCard for equal-height columns.

### Snackbars / Notifications

- Success alerts appear at the **top of the screen** in **Backstage green**.
- Auto-dismiss — no manual close ("X") button.
- Copy pattern: "Scorecard {name} refreshed" / "Could not refresh {name}. Try again."
- For refresh actions: show tooltip "Refresh"; after refresh, tooltip becomes "Just now".

### Card Actions — Kebab Menu

When a card has more than 2 actions, consolidate them into a **kebab menu** (vertical three-dot icon button) in the card's top-right corner. Never render more than 2 visible action buttons on a card surface.

```tsx
// Kebab trigger
<IconButton aria-label="More actions" onClick={handleMenuOpen}>
  <MoreVertIcon />
</IconButton>

// Menu items (MUI Menu)
<MenuItem onClick={handleEdit}>Edit</MenuItem>
<MenuItem onClick={handleDuplicate}>Duplicate</MenuItem>
<Divider />
<MenuItem onClick={handleDelete} sx={{ color: 'error.main' }}>Delete</MenuItem>
```

- The kebab `IconButton` must have `aria-label="More actions"`.
- Destructive actions (Delete, Remove) go last, separated by a `Divider`, and use `color: 'error.main'`.
- Tooltip the kebab trigger: "More actions".

### Chat Interfaces — Lightspeed UI

For any chat or conversational AI interface in RHDH, use **`@patternfly/chatbot`** (the Lightspeed UI component library). Do not build custom chat UIs.

- Scope all `@patternfly/chatbot` CSS inside an isolation wrapper to prevent global style bleed (see PatternFly scoping rule above).
- Supported modes: full-screen, docked (side panel), overlay/modal.
- Follow the PF Chatbot component API for message threading, input, and action buttons.

### CRUD

- Full Create / Read / Update / Delete for any admin-facing resource.
- Delete always requires a confirmation modal:
  - Title: "Delete [resource name]?"
  - Body: "This action cannot be undone."
  - Buttons: "Cancel" (secondary) + "Delete" (destructive/danger variant) — never just "OK".
  - The confirm button must be styled as destructive (red / `color="error"`).

### Creation Flows

Choose the right container based on complexity:

| Flow complexity | Pattern |
|---|---|
| **Complex** (multiple steps, many fields, wizards) | Full-page **Software Templates UI** (Backstage Create page experience with `Stepper`) |
| **Simple** (≤ 5 fields, single step) | **Modal** or **Side Drawer** |

- Mirror the Backstage Software Templates / Create page layout and step structure for complex flows.
- Use a **side drawer** (MUI `Drawer` anchor="right") for forms that benefit from keeping context visible behind them.
- Use a **modal** for quick, self-contained forms.
- **Never stack two modals on top of each other.** If a flow needs a secondary dialog while a modal is open (e.g. a confirmation inside a form), close the modal first or use a **side drawer as the primary container** and open the confirmation as a modal on top of the drawer.

### Edit Flows

Edit flows must be **identical to the creation flow** in layout and field structure, with these changes:

- **Title:** "Edit [name of what is being edited]" — e.g. "Edit Scorecard" or "Edit Workflow".
- **Submit button:** "Save" — not "Create", "Add", or "Submit".
- **Pre-populated:** All form fields must be pre-filled with the current values so the user edits only what they need to change.
- Reuse the same form component as the creation flow — do not build a separate edit form.

---

## Microcopy and UX Writing

Follow [PatternFly capitalization guidelines](https://www.patternfly.org/ux-writing/capitalization).

| Rule | Example |
|------|---------|
| Navigation labels: Title Case | "Learning Paths", "My Plugins", "Skill Marketplace" |
| Button labels: Title Case | "Add Member", "Save Changes", "Create Bundle" |
| Column headers: Title Case | "Last Updated", "Created By", "Actions" |
| Body copy / descriptions: Sentence case | "No plugins added yet." |
| Error messages: Sentence case, actionable | "Could not load plugins. Try refreshing the page." |
| Terminology: "Member" / "Members" | Never "User" / "Users" in RHDH interfaces |
| Tooltip for refresh | "Refresh" → "Just now" after action |

**Never use:** "leverage", "synergy", "unlock", "seamlessly", "intuitive", "north star".
**Prefer:** short, direct, plain language that describes exactly what happened or what to do.

### Tooltips on Icon Buttons

Every icon-only button must have both an `aria-label` **and** a visible `Tooltip` that shows the button's label on hover. Never rely on `aria-label` alone for discoverability.

```tsx
<Tooltip title="Refresh">
  <IconButton aria-label="Refresh" onClick={handleRefresh}>
    <RefreshIcon />
  </IconButton>
</Tooltip>
```

- Tooltip text must match the `aria-label` exactly.
- Use MUI `Tooltip` (or PatternFly `Tooltip` if inside a PF-scoped section).
- Placement: default (`bottom`) unless the button is at the bottom of the viewport.

### Truncated Text — Tooltip on Hover

Any text that can be truncated with an ellipsis (`...`) must show the full value in a `Tooltip` on hover. Never truncate silently.

```tsx
<Tooltip title={fullName}>
  <Typography noWrap sx={{ maxWidth: 200, overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis' }}>
    {fullName}
  </Typography>
</Tooltip>
```

This applies to: card titles, table cell values, chip labels, sidebar nav items, and any other text constrained to a fixed width. Test with names longer than 40 characters to verify.

---

## Navigation and Branding

- **Sidebar labels:** Title Case.
- **Sidebar icons:** Always use the **outlined** style variant of Material Design icons (e.g. `HomeOutlined`, `SettingsOutlined`, `ExtensionOutlined`) — never the filled/default variant. This matches the RHDH navigation visual style.
- **RHDH logo:** Replace the Backstage logo in the main sidebar navigation with the official **Red Hat Developer Hub logo**:
  [Logo-Red_Hat-Developer_Hub-A-Standard-RGB.svg](https://redhat.brand-portal.adobe.com/assetdetails.html/content/dam/mac/redhat/brand/logos/product-logos/red-hat-developer-hub/Logo-Red_Hat-Developer_Hub-A-Standard-RGB.svg)
  Preserve aspect ratio — never distort or stretch the logo.
- **Breadcrumbs:** For all internal plugin pages, always use the Backstage `Header` breadcrumb pattern — the `type` and `typeLink` props render above the page title automatically. Never use a custom back button or build a separate breadcrumb row.
- **Catalog entity links:** Any component name, entity name, or catalog reference shown in the UI must be a **link to the entity's catalog overview page** (`/catalog/:namespace/:kind/:name`). Never display entity names as plain unlinked text.
- **Tabs:** `HeaderTabs` for dynamic routed tabs; `TabbedLayout` for static tab panes.
- **Tab counters:** When a tab displays a list or card grid, append the item count to the tab label: `"Skills (12)"`. If the count is zero, show **no counter** and render an empty state inside the tab instead.

```tsx
// Tab label with count
const label = count > 0 ? `Skills (${count})` : 'Skills';
```

---

## Dark Theme

RHDH supports both light and dark themes. Every plugin must work correctly in both.

- **Never hardcode colors.** Use MUI theme tokens (`theme.palette.background.default`, `theme.palette.text.primary`, `theme.palette.divider`, etc.) — never raw hex or RGB values.
- **Never hardcode `#ffffff` or `#000000`** for text or backgrounds.
- Use `theme.palette.mode === 'dark'` for conditional styling only when a component genuinely needs different treatment per theme.
- Test every page and component in **both light and dark mode** before marking it done. Toggle via Backstage sidebar settings in local dev.
- Backstage core components and status components are already theme-aware — prefer them over custom-colored elements.
- For illustrations or images, provide both-theme-compatible versions, or use CSS `filter: invert(1)` with caution.

```tsx
// Correct — theme-aware
sx={{ color: 'text.primary', bgcolor: 'background.paper' }}

// Wrong — hardcoded
sx={{ color: '#1a1a1a', bgcolor: '#ffffff' }}
```

---

## Prototype Publishing

When a prototype is complete, **always remind the user to add it to the team's public prototype hub:**

> Add this prototype to [AppDev UX Prototypes](https://yuvalgalanti.github.io/AppDev-UX-Prototypes/) — include the prototype name, a short description, the live URL, and a VPN label if access requires VPN.

The hub is the canonical reference for engineering and stakeholders. All team prototypes live there (Lightspeed, Orchestrator, Scorecards, Learning Paths, DCM, Podman Desktop, etc.).

---

## Plugin Structure Conventions

```
plugins/my-plugin/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── MyPluginPage.tsx        # Top-level routed page
│   │   ├── MyPluginPage.test.tsx
│   │   ├── SomeFeature/
│   │   │   ├── SomeFeatureCard.tsx
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   └── index.ts
│   ├── hooks/                      # Data fetching hooks
│   ├── api/                        # API client and types
│   └── index.ts
├── package.json
└── README.md
```

- One routed page component per top-level route; sub-views use `HeaderTabs`.
- API calls go in hooks or an API class — not inline in components.
- Export through `index.ts` — don't deep-import across plugin boundaries.
- After local Backstage implementation, export with `npx @janus-idp/cli@latest package export-dynamic-plugin` and verify in local RHDH.

### GitLab Pages Prototype Setup

Every GitLab Pages prototype must implement these three things. See [gitlab-pages.md](gitlab-pages.md) for full code examples.

1. **Bypass the sign-in screen** — use the guest `SignInPage` component or set `auth.environment: development` in `app-config.pages.yaml`. Users must land directly on the plugin, never on the login screen.

2. **Unregister service workers** — add a `navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations()` unregister snippet to `public/index.html`. This prevents stale cached builds from persisting after a new deploy, so reviewers always see the latest version without clearing their browser.

3. **Static backend for catalog items** — GitLab Pages has no live backend. Serve catalog entities from `public/static-catalog/entities.json` using `fetch()` in hooks instead of `useApi(catalogApiRef)`. Keep the entity shape identical to the real Backstage catalog API. Never hardcode mock data inside component files.

---

### GitLab CI/CD

When creating or modifying any `.gitlab-ci.yml` file for GitLab Pages or CI/CD pipelines, **every job must include `tags: [itup-alm-x86]`**:

```yaml
deploy:
  tags:
    - itup-alm-x86
  script:
    - ...
```

### Developer Source Handoff

Upon completion of the implementation phase, package the source code into `Downloads/[name]-source/` with the following structure:

**Required assets:**
- `src/` — TypeScript source only (no compiled output)
- `package.json`
- `tsconfig.json`
- `README.md`
- `DEV-SETUP.md`
- `HOW-TO-VIEW.md`
- `QUICK-START.md`

**Exclude:** compiled build artifacts and `node_modules/`.

---

## Accessibility

- All interactive elements must have visible focus styles and keyboard access.
- Icon-only buttons must have `aria-label`.
- Tables must have column headers (`<th scope="col">`).
- Color must never be the only way to convey status — pair with an icon or text label. Use Backstage status components (`StatusOK`, etc.) which are already accessible.
- Minimum touch target: 44×44px.
- Test keyboard-only navigation before considering any interactive component done.
- Status icons in tab labels (e.g. warning icons) — use Material `WarningAmber`, paired with visible text, not icon-only.

---

## Anti-Patterns

| Anti-pattern | Correct approach |
|---|---|
| Import `patternfly.min.css` in a plugin | Use PF primitives only; no global CSS imports |
| Inject tokens on `document.documentElement` | Never; use MUI theme or local CSS-in-JS |
| Custom tab components | `HeaderTabs` or `TabbedLayout` |
| Custom back button | `Header` `type` + `typeLink` |
| Bespoke card/tile components | `ItemCardGrid` + MUI `Card` |
| `StatusDot` or color-only status | Backstage `StatusOK` / `StatusError` etc. |
| Static CRA mocks inside a real plugin | Real `useApi` + Backstage API refs |
| Assume RHDH top bar exists locally | Only present in deployed RHDH |
| Color as sole status indicator | Always icon + label + color |
| Spinner for page-level loading | Skeleton screens (MUI `Skeleton`) |
| More than 2 visible action buttons on a card | Kebab menu (`MoreVertIcon` + MUI `Menu`) |
| Custom chat UI | `@patternfly/chatbot` (Lightspeed UI), scoped |
| Icon-only button with no tooltip | Always wrap in `<Tooltip title="...">` |
| Truncated text with no tooltip | Wrap truncated text in `<Tooltip title={fullValue}>` |
| Delete modal with "OK" or "Confirm" button | Use "Delete" (destructive, red) + "Cancel" |
| Left-aligned or top-aligned empty state | Center-align both horizontally and vertically |
| Two modals stacked on top of each other | Side drawer as primary, modal as secondary |
| Complex creation flow in a modal | Use Software Templates / Create page UI |
| Edit form title as "Create …" or no title | "Edit [name]" with "Save" button, pre-populated |
| Custom filter dropdowns / inline filter rows | Backstage catalog-style filter sidebar |
| Table action buttons above or below the table | Place next to search bar via `Table` `actions` prop |
| Table title with no item count | Always show count in title: "Roles (4)" |
| Entity name as plain text | Link to catalog entity overview page |
| Hardcoded hex colors | MUI theme tokens (`theme.palette.*`) |
| No dark theme testing | Test in both light and dark mode |
| Tab shows "(0)" counter | Show no counter; render empty state in tab body |
| Custom back button | `Header` `type` + `typeLink` breadcrumb |
| Backstage logo in sidebar | RHDH Red Hat Developer Hub logo |
| Filled/default icons in sidebar nav | Use outlined icon variants (e.g. `HomeOutlined`) |
| Custom icon/image with no source link | Make it clickable; open source URL in new tab |

## Design Review Checklist

**States**
- [ ] Loading uses skeleton screens (MUI `Skeleton`), not just a spinner
- [ ] Error uses `<WarningPanel />` with real error message
- [ ] Empty uses `<EmptyState />` — center-aligned, with one action
- [ ] Tabs with zero items show no counter and render an empty state

**Components**
- [ ] Tabs use `HeaderTabs` or `TabbedLayout`, not custom
- [ ] Tabs with content show item count: "Skills (12)"
- [ ] Tile/card views use `ItemCardGrid` + MUI `Card`
- [ ] Cards use `InfoCard` with controls in `action` prop
- [ ] Cards with > 2 actions use a kebab menu (`MoreVertIcon`)
- [ ] Chat UI uses `@patternfly/chatbot` (Lightspeed UI), scoped
- [ ] Rich text uses `MarkdownContent`
- [ ] Key-value metadata uses `StructuredMetadataTable`
- [ ] Status uses Backstage `StatusOK` / `StatusError` etc. (not custom chips)
- [ ] Icons are from Material Design or PatternFly only
- [ ] Custom icons/images outside MUI/PF are clickable with source URL

**Filters & Tables**
- [ ] Filters use Backstage catalog-style filter sidebar
- [ ] Table title includes item count: "Roles (4)", updates reactively
- [ ] Table action buttons are placed next to the search bar (via `actions` prop)
- [ ] Catalog entity names are linked to their catalog overview page

**Microcopy**
- [ ] Nav and button labels are Title Case
- [ ] Body copy and errors are Sentence case
- [ ] "Member" used instead of "User"
- [ ] Actions column is named "Actions"
- [ ] No jargon ("leverage", "seamlessly", etc.)

**Tooltips**
- [ ] Every icon-only button has both `aria-label` and a `Tooltip`
- [ ] Tooltip text matches `aria-label` exactly
- [ ] Every truncated text (`...`) has a `Tooltip` showing the full value
- [ ] Long-name scenario tested with 40+ character strings

**Creation & Edit Flows**
- [ ] Complex flows use the Software Templates / Create page UI
- [ ] Simple flows (≤ 5 fields) use modal or side drawer
- [ ] No two modals stacked — use side drawer + modal instead
- [ ] Edit flows reuse creation form, title is "Edit [name]", button is "Save"
- [ ] Edit form fields are pre-populated with current values

**Behavior**
- [ ] Delete has a confirmation modal with "Cancel" + destructive "Delete" button
- [ ] Destructive confirm button is styled red / `color="error"`
- [ ] Success snackbar: top of screen, green, auto-dismiss, no close X
- [ ] Tables have pagination (if > 10–15 items)
- [ ] No global PatternFly CSS imported
- [ ] No token injection on document root
- [ ] All data fetching is in hooks/API layer

**Branding & Navigation**
- [ ] RHDH logo used (not Backstage logo), aspect ratio preserved
- [ ] Sidebar nav icons use outlined style (e.g. `HomeOutlined`, not `Home`)
- [ ] Breadcrumbs use `Header` `type` + `typeLink` (above the page title)
- [ ] No custom back buttons

**Dark Theme**
- [ ] No hardcoded colors — all use MUI theme tokens
- [ ] Tested in both light and dark mode

**Prototype**
- [ ] User reminded to publish to [AppDev UX Prototypes hub](https://yuvalgalanti.github.io/AppDev-UX-Prototypes/)

**Accessibility**
- [ ] Icon-only buttons have `aria-label` + visible tooltip
- [ ] Color is not the sole status indicator
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works on all interactive elements
